questions.question: How had the mice organized themselves in the house?
options: They lived alone and avoided each other
They created a sophisticated society with scouts, gatherers, and elders
They only came out during the day

questions.question: How did the mice feel about their situation?
options: Confident and perhaps a bit careless

questions.question: Why did the house owners bring in the cat?
options: To help control what they saw as a mouse problem
To catch birds in the garden

questions.question: How did the cat's arrival change life for the mice?
options: It made no difference at all
Their safe home became a dangerous territory

questions.question: Why did the mice call an emergency council meeting?
options: Because they faced a desperate situation with the cat

questions.question: What did the eldest mouse say about this cat?
options: That she was friendly and harmless
That she was a dedicated hunter, unlike lazy house cats

questions.question: What was the young mouse's solution?
options: To move to a different house
To put a bell around the cat's neck for early warning
To make friends with the cat

questions.question: How did the other mice react to this proposal?
options: They became excited and thought it was brilliant

questions.question: What important question did the old mouse ask?
options: Where can we buy a bell?
Who exactly is going to put the bell around the cat's neck?
What kind of bell should they use?

questions.question: How did the mice react to this question?
options: Their excitement turned to stunned silence

questions.question: Why couldn't the mice carry out their plan?
options: No mouse was willing to risk their life approaching the dangerous cat

questions.question: What lesson did the mice learn?
options: That good ideas are only valuable if they can actually be put into practice

informationalTexts.title: A House Full of Mice
informationalTexts.content:
Learning about A House Full of Mice is interesting and important for second graders. There are many fascinating facts about A House Full of Mice that help us understand the world better. By studying different topics, students build knowledge and develop curiosity.

When we explore A House Full of Mice, we make connections to other subjects and our daily lives. Teachers and books can help us learn more about A House Full of Mice. The more we learn, the better we understand how things work and why they matter.

questions.question: What can you learn about A House Full of Mice?
options: fascinating facts that help us understand the world

questions.question: Why is it good to learn about A House Full of Mice?
options: it helps us understand the world better

grammarLessons.content:
**Nouns**

This lesson focuses on Nouns. As you read today's story, pay attention to examples of this concept.

**Key Points:**
• Understand the concept
• Find examples in the story
• Practice using it correctly

**Practice:**
Apply what you learned by completing the exercises.

**Remember:** Nouns is an important language skill!
grammarLessons.objective: Students will understand and apply Nouns.

**ABC Order**

This language lesson teaches ABC Order. Look for examples in today's story.

**Key Ideas:**
• Learn the concept
• Find examples
• Practice the skill

**Practice Activities:**
Complete the exercises to master this skill.

**Remember:** Practice makes perfect!
languageLessons.objective: Students will learn about ABC Order.

languageLessons.content:
Rhyming words sound the SAME at the end. They make poems and stories fun to read aloud!

**Rhyming Words:**
• cat, hat, mat, sat (all end with -at)
• play, day, say, way (all end with -ay)
• book, look, cook, took (all end with -ook)

**From today's story:**
Listen for words that rhyme as you read!

**Practice - Find the rhyme:**
1. Which word rhymes with "dog"? (log, big, cat)
2. Which word rhymes with "jump"? (run, bump, sit)
3. Which word rhymes with "bed"? (chair, red, sleep)

**Remember:** Listen to the END of words to find rhymes!
languageLessons.objective: Students will identify and create rhyming words.
